v1AgentSessionStatus.description:
AgentSessionStatus represents the abstract state of the AI agent running in a session.
Provider-agnostic: works for any AI agent (Claude, etc.), not tied to a specific provider.

 - AGENT_SESSION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: No agent activity or agent not configured.
 - AGENT_SESSION_STATUS_WORKING: Agent is actively processing.
 - AGENT_SESSION_STATUS_WAITING_FOR_INPUT: Agent is waiting for user input (permission prompt, elicitation, etc.).
 - AGENT_SESSION_STATUS_IDLE: Agent finished processing and is idle (waiting at the prompt).

v1AutoMappedInput.description:
AutoMappedInput records a template session input that was auto-filled from
the caller's saved inputs because map_saved_to_session_inputs was set.

savedInputId.description: ID of the saved input used to populate the value.

sessionInputKey.description: Key of the template session input that was auto-filled.

v1ClusterOption.description: ClusterOption represents a single cluster that can serve a given image + machine type.

v1CompareSessionTemplateResponse.description: CompareSessionTemplateResponse contains both the snapshot and current template configurations.

changedVariableKeys.description:
Keys of template variables whose values differ between snapshot and current.
Only the keys are returned; actual values are never exposed.

current.description: Current template configuration (null if the template is deleted).

snapshot.description: Template configuration from the session's snapshot (at creation time).

v1CreateSavedInputRequest.description: CreateSavedInputRequest is the request for creating a saved input.

isSecret.description: Whether this is a secret value.

v1CreateSavedInputResponse.description: CreateSavedInputResponse is the response for creating a saved input.

v1CreateSessionResponse.description: CreateSessionResponse is the response for creating a session.

autoMappedInputs.description:
Session inputs that were auto-filled from the user's saved inputs.
Empty unless the request set map_saved_to_session_inputs=true.

v1CreateTemplateResponse.description: CreateTemplateResponse is the response for creating a template.

v1DeleteSavedInputResponse.description: DeleteSavedInputResponse is the response for deleting a saved input.

v1DeleteSessionResponse.description: DeleteSessionResponse is the response for deleting a session.

v1DeleteTemplateResponse.description: DeleteTemplateResponse is the response for deleting a template.

v1DeleteTerminatedSessionsResponse.description: DeleteTerminatedSessionsResponse is the response for deleting all terminated sessions.

deletedCount.description: Number of sessions that were deleted.

v1FeatureFlag.description: FeatureFlag represents a toggleable feature for a template.

description.description: Description explaining what this feature flag enables.

v1FeatureFlagCreate.description: FeatureFlagCreate is used when creating a feature flag.

description.description: Description explaining what this feature flag enables.

v1GetMeResponse.description: GetMeResponse is the response containing the current authenticated user.

v1GetSavedInputResponse.description: GetSavedInputResponse is the response for getting a saved input.

v1GetSessionLogsResponse.description: GetSessionLogsResponse is a single log chunk in the stream.

heartbeatMessage.description: Whether this is a heartbeat message (empty content, just keeping connection alive).

v1GetSessionResponse.description: GetSessionResponse is the response for getting a session.

v1GetTemplateResponse.description: GetTemplateResponse is the response for getting a template.

v1GetWorkspaceInfoResponse.description:
GetWorkspaceInfoResponse carries workspace-level capabilities the UI uses to
tailor what it shows.

isFreeTrial.description:
Whether the workspace is on a free trial. Free-trial sessions are pinned to
an 8-hour auto-terminate window that cannot be changed.

v1Image.description:
Image represents a machine image available for sessions.

Deprecated: use Stack. Images expose opaque merged-stack ids (e.g. "osx-26-edge")
that bundle many Xcode versions and can be recomposed at any time. Stack exposes
the stable, specific stack id with friendly metadata.

clusterName.description: The cluster where this image is available.

isDefault.description:
Whether this is the deployment's default image. Clients preselect it when
the user has made no prior choice. At most one image name is marked.

v1ListImagesResponse.description: ListImagesResponse is the response for listing images.

v1ListMachineTypesResponse.description: ListMachineTypesResponse is the response for listing machine types.

v1ListSavedInputsResponse.description: ListSavedInputsResponse is the response for listing saved inputs.

v1ListSessionNotificationsResponse.description: ListSessionNotificationsResponse is the response for listing session notifications.

v1ListSessionsResponse.description: ListSessionsResponse is the response for listing sessions.

v1ListStacksResponse.description: ListStacksResponse is the response for listing stacks.

v1ListTemplatesResponse.description: ListTemplatesResponse is the response for listing templates.

v1MachineType.description: MachineType represents a machine type available for sessions.

clusterName.description: The cluster where this machine type is available.

isDefault.description:
Whether this is the deployment's default machine type. Clients preselect it
when the user has made no prior choice. At most one machine type name is
marked.

title.description:
Human-friendly title, e.g. "M2 Pro Large". Empty when no friendly metadata
is available.

v1OpenRemoteAccessResponse.description: OpenRemoteAccessResponse is the response for opening remote access.

session.description: The updated session with remote access details.

v1PersistentDiskStatus.description:
PersistentDiskStatus represents the status of a machine's persistent disk.

 - PERSISTENT_DISK_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified or not applicable (non-terminated sessions).
 - PERSISTENT_DISK_STATUS_AVAILABLE: The disk is available and the session can be restored.
 - PERSISTENT_DISK_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE_SOON: The disk will become unavailable soon (within 1 week).
The session can still be restored but user should be warned.
 - PERSISTENT_DISK_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE: The disk is no longer available. The session cannot be restored.

v1ResolveClustersResponse.description:
ResolveClustersResponse contains the list of clusters that offer both the
requested image and machine type.

clusters.description: Matching clusters (one entry per cluster that has both image and machine type).

v1RestoreSessionResponse.description: RestoreSessionResponse is the response for restoring a session.

v1SavedInput.description:
SavedInput represents a saved credential/value for a user.
Users can reuse these across sessions (e.g., GitHub PAT, SSH keys).

isSecret.description: Whether this is a secret value.

value.description:
Value of the input. For secret inputs this is empty unless the request explicitly
set include_secrets=true (see GetSavedInputRequest/ListSavedInputsRequest).

v1Session.description: Session represents a remote development environment instance.

agentSessionStatus.description:
Current abstract status of the AI agent running in this session.
Provider-agnostic (works for any AI agent). Updated via webhook notifications.

agentSessionStatusUpdatedAt.description: When the agent session status was last updated.

aiConfigured.description:
Whether this session's VM was set up with Claude Code AI tooling.
If ai_enabled is true but ai_configured is false, the session needs a
restart to install Claude Code.

aiEnabled.description: Whether Claude Code AI is available (user has ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configured).

aiPrompt.description: AI prompt passed to Claude Code when the session was created.

autoTerminateAt.description: When the session will auto-terminate. Empty if disabled or already processed by the worker.

autoTerminateMinutes.description: The user's chosen auto-terminate duration in minutes. 0 if disabled.

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value metadata attached to the session (e.g. team,
branch, ticket id). Returned verbatim; filterable via
ListSessionsRequest.label_selectors.

persistentDiskStatus.description:
Status of the persistent disk for terminated sessions.
Used to determine if a session can be restored.

sshConnectionOpen.description: Whether the SSH connection is fully established and ready.

templateDeleted.description: Whether the template this session was created from has been deleted.

templateId.description: ID of the template this session was created from (historical reference).

templateOutdated.description:
Whether the template has been updated since this session was created.
Computed by comparing the snapshot's updated_at with the current template's updated_at.
False when the template is deleted or the snapshot predates this feature.

templateSnapshot.description:
Snapshot of the template configuration at session creation time.
Contains all template variables, session input values, feature flags, and workspace links.

updatedAt.description: When the session was last updated.

v1SessionClickResponse.description: SessionClickResponse is the response for clicking.

v1SessionCompleteFileUploadResponse.description: SessionCompleteFileUploadResponse is the response for completing an upload.

v1SessionCompleteUploadResponse.description:
SessionCompleteUploadResponse is the response for completing an upload.

Deprecated: use SessionCompleteFileUploadResponse.

v1SessionDownloadFileResponse.description: SessionDownloadFileResponse is the response for downloading files.

signedUrl.description: GCS signed GET URL for the tar.gz archive.

v1SessionDownloadResponse.description:
SessionDownloadResponse is the response for downloading files.

Deprecated: use SessionDownloadFileResponse.

signedUrl.description: GCS signed GET URL for the tar.gz archive.

v1SessionExecuteResponse.description: SessionExecuteResponse is the response for executing a command.

output.description: Combined stdout/stderr output of the command.

v1SessionInput.description:
SessionInput defines an input that users must or may provide when creating a session.
Configured by the template author. Filled by the session starter.

defaultValue.description:
Default value used in boot scripts when the session starter leaves the field blank.
Only applicable when required is false.

description.description: Description explaining what this input is for.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether to expose this input as an ENV VAR in interactive terminal sessions.

key.description: Key/name of the input (matches saved_inputs.key for auto-prefill).

required.description: Whether this input is required. If false, the input is optional and default_value is used when not provided.

v1SessionInputCreate.description: SessionInputCreate is used when creating a session input definition on a template.

defaultValue.description: Default value when not provided by the session starter. Only used when required is false.

description.description: Description explaining what this input is for.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether to expose as an ENV VAR in interactive terminal sessions.

required.description: Whether this input is required. Defaults to false (optional).

v1SessionInputValue.description:
SessionInputValue provides a value for a session input when creating a session.
Either provide a direct value or reference a saved input by ID (for secrets).

isSecret.description: Whether the value is secret (encrypt at rest). Ignored if saved_input_id is set.

key.description: Key name matching a session input on the template.

savedInputId.description:
Optional: ID of a Saved Input to use. Backend fetches and decrypts the value server-side.
This avoids exposing secret values to the frontend.

value.description: Direct value (used when user typed in a value). Ignored if saved_input_id is set.

v1SessionMouseDragResponse.description: SessionMouseDragResponse is the response for dragging the mouse.

v1SessionNotification.description: SessionNotification represents a persisted notification from a VM.

v1SessionNotificationType.description:
SessionNotificationType defines the type of notification from the VM.

 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified notification type.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_STOP: The AI agent has stopped and is waiting for user input.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_PERMISSION_PROMPT: The AI agent is requesting permission to proceed.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_IDLE_PROMPT: The AI agent is idle and waiting for a prompt.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_AUTH_SUCCESS: The AI agent has successfully authenticated.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_ELICITATION_DIALOG: The AI agent is showing an elicitation dialog.
 - SESSION_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_AGENT_WORKING: The AI agent is actively working (status-only signal, no notification record created).

v1SessionScreenshotResponse.description: SessionScreenshotResponse is the response for taking a screenshot.

signedUrl.description: GCS signed GET URL for the JPEG screenshot image.

v1SessionScrollResponse.description: SessionScrollResponse is the response for scrolling.

v1SessionStartFileUploadResponse.description: SessionStartFileUploadResponse is the response for initiating an upload.

signedUrl.description: GCS signed PUT URL for uploading the tar.gz archive.

uploadId.description: Upload identifier to use when completing the upload.

v1SessionStartUploadResponse.description:
SessionStartUploadResponse is the response for initiating an upload.

Deprecated: use SessionStartFileUploadResponse.

signedUrl.description: GCS signed PUT URL for uploading the tar.gz archive.

uploadId.description: Upload identifier to use when completing the upload.

v1SessionStatus.description:
SessionStatus represents the current state of a session.

 - SESSION_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified status.
 - SESSION_STATUS_PENDING: Session is being created/provisioned.
 - SESSION_STATUS_STARTING: Session is starting up.
 - SESSION_STATUS_RUNNING: Session is running and accessible.
 - SESSION_STATUS_TERMINATING: Session is terminating.
 - SESSION_STATUS_FAILED: Session creation or operation failed.
 - SESSION_STATUS_TERMINATED: Session is terminated (machine no longer running).
 - SESSION_STATUS_DRAINING: Session VM is being drained off its node (drain in progress, VM still consuming resources).
 - SESSION_STATUS_DRAINED: Session VM was drained and is gone; the session may be restarted to recreate it.
 - SESSION_STATUS_UNKNOWN: The machine status is unknown, most likely because its node lost network
connectivity. Lifecycle operations (start, delete) are blocked until the
state settles.

v1SessionTemplateSnapshot.description:
SessionTemplateSnapshot contains the template configuration snapshotted at session creation time.
This makes sessions self-contained and resilient to template changes or deletions.

featureFlags.description: Feature flag states for this session.

hasStartupScript.description: Whether the template had a startup script configured.

hasWarmupScript.description: Whether the template had a warmup script configured.

image.description:
Machine image name from the template.

Superseded by stack_id (prefer it). The image is the resolved merged-stack
id; stack_id is the stable, specific stack the session runs.

machineType.description: Machine type name from the template.

sessionInputs.description: Session input values provided by the session starter (or defaults).

stackId.description:
Stack id this session runs (e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge"). Empty for legacy
sessions snapshotted before stacks existed (they carry image only).

templateName.description: Name of the template at creation time.

updatedAt.description:
When the template was last updated (captured at session creation time).
Used to detect if the template has been modified since this session was created.
Absent for sessions created before this feature was added.

workspaceLinks.description: Workspace links applicable to this session (filtered by enabled feature flags at creation time).

v1SessionTypeResponse.description: SessionTypeResponse is the response for typing.

v1SnapshotFlag.description: SnapshotFlag represents a feature flag state captured at session creation time.

v1SnapshotInput.description: SnapshotInput represents a session input value captured at session creation time.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether this value is exposed as an ENV VAR in interactive terminal sessions.

isSecret.description: Whether this is a secret value.

value.description:
Value of the input. For secret inputs this is empty unless the request explicitly
set include_secrets=true (see GetSessionRequest).

v1SnapshotLink.description: SnapshotLink represents a workspace link captured at session creation time.

v1Stack.description:
Stack is a provisionable development environment stack with human-friendly
metadata (title, OS, status, Xcode version). The id is the stable identifier
stored on a template/session.

clusterNames.description: The clusters where this stack can be provisioned.

descriptionLink.description: Link to the stack's pre-installed tools / system report.

id.description: Stack id, e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge". Stored on the template/session.

isDefault.description:
Whether this is the deployment's default stack. Clients preselect it when
the user has made no prior choice. At most one stack id is marked.

osVersion.description: Numeric OS version (e.g. 26 for macOS, 24 for Ubuntu 24.04).

xcodeVersion.description: Xcode version (e.g. "16.0"); empty for non-Xcode stacks. Informational.

v1StartSessionResponse.description:
StartSessionResponse is the response for starting a session.

Deprecated: use RestoreSessionResponse.

v1StopSessionResponse.description:
StopSessionResponse is the response for stopping a session.

Deprecated: use TerminateSessionResponse.

v1Template.description: Template defines a reusable configuration for creating sessions.

createdByEmail.description: Email of the user who created this template (owner).

description.description: Description explaining what this template is for.

featureFlags.description: Feature flags available for this template.

image.description:
Machine image name (e.g., "osx-xcode-edge"). Consistent across clusters.

Superseded by stack_id (prefer it). The image is a merged-stack id that
bundles many Xcode versions; stack_id is the stable, specific contract.

machineType.description: Machine type name (e.g., "g2.mac.m2pro.4c"). Consistent across clusters.

sessionInputs.description: Session inputs that users must or may provide when creating a session.

stackId.description:
Stack id (e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge"). The stable contract stored on the
template; resolved to a provisionable image at session-start time.

startupScript.description: Startup script (bash) that runs every time the session starts or restarts.

templateVariables.description: Template variables baked into this template (available in boot scripts).

updatedAt.description: When the template was last updated.

warmupScript.description:
Warmup script (bash) that runs only once during initial session creation.
Use for setup tasks like dependency installation.

workingDirectory.description:
Working directory for terminal sessions. If set, terminals start in this directory
instead of the home directory. Must be an absolute path.

workspaceId.description: Workspace ID that this template is associated with.

workspaceLinks.description: Workspace links configured for this template.

v1TemplateConfig.description:
TemplateConfig contains the full template configuration for comparison.
Used by CompareSessionTemplate to show what changed between snapshot and current template.

sessionInputs.description: Session input definitions (keys and metadata, not actual values).

stackId.description:
Stack id (e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge"). The stable contract; distinguishes
stack-only changes that resolve to the same provisionable image.

templateVariables.description: Template variable keys and metadata (secret values stripped).

updatedAt.description: When the template was last updated.

v1TemplateConfigFlag.description: TemplateConfigFlag represents a feature flag for comparison.

enabled.description: Whether this flag is enabled by default.

v1TemplateConfigInput.description: TemplateConfigInput represents a session input definition for comparison.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether this input is exposed as an environment variable.

isSecret.description: Whether this input is a secret.

v1TemplateConfigVariable.description:
TemplateConfigVariable represents a template variable for comparison.
Secret values are never exposed — only key and metadata.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether this variable is exposed as an environment variable.

isSecret.description: Whether this variable is a secret.

v1TemplateVariable.description:
TemplateVariable represents a baked-in variable for a template.
Available in boot scripts. Optionally exposed as an ENV VAR in terminal sessions.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether to expose this variable as an ENV VAR in interactive terminal sessions.

isSecret.description: Whether this is a secret value.

value.description:
Value of the variable. For secret variables this is empty unless the request
explicitly set include_secrets=true (see GetTemplateRequest/ListTemplatesRequest).

v1TemplateVariableCreate.description: TemplateVariableCreate is used when creating a template variable.

exposeAsEnvVar.description: Whether to expose as an ENV VAR in interactive terminal sessions.

isSecret.description: Whether this is a secret value.

v1TerminateSessionResponse.description: TerminateSessionResponse is the response for terminating a session.

v1UpdateSavedInputResponse.description: UpdateSavedInputResponse is the response for updating a saved input.

v1UpdateSessionResponse.description: UpdateSessionResponse is the response for updating a session.

v1UpdateTemplateResponse.description: UpdateTemplateResponse is the response for updating a template.

v1User.description: User represents an authenticated user in the system.

createdAt.description: When the user was first seen.

updatedAt.description: When the user record was last updated.

v1WorkspaceLink.description: WorkspaceLink represents a configured IDE workspace folder link for a template.

featureFlagId.description: Optional feature flag ID that controls visibility.

label.description: Display label for the button (e.g., "iOS App").

v1WorkspaceLinkCreate.description: WorkspaceLinkCreate is used when creating a workspace link.

featureFlagName.description: Optional: name of the feature flag that controls visibility.

info.description:
API for managing Bitrise Remote Dev Environments (RDE).

> **⚠️ Experimental API.** **Breaking changes may be introduced at any time without notice or a deprecation period.**

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bitrise Personal Access Token (PAT) sent as a bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: token <YOUR_PAT>
```

Generate a PAT from your Bitrise account settings.

## Base URL

Production: `https://api.bitrise.io/rde`

## Errors

Non-2xx responses share a single JSON envelope:

```
{
  "code": 5,
  "message": "template not found",
  "details": []
}
```

The `code` field is a numeric status code (not the HTTP status code). Clients should switch on `code` for programmatic handling and surface `message` to humans. Common values:

| HTTP | `code` | Name               | When                                                          |
| ---: | -----: | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  400 |      3 | InvalidArgument    | Request validation failed; per-field violations may appear in `details`. |
|  401 |     16 | Unauthenticated    | Missing or invalid `Authorization` header / PAT.              |
|  403 |      7 | PermissionDenied   | The PAT does not have access to the requested workspace or resource. |
|  404 |      5 | NotFound           | The resource does not exist (or is not visible to this PAT). |
|  409 |     10 | Aborted            | Request conflicts with the current resource state (e.g. session already running). |
|  412 |      9 | FailedPrecondition | A precondition for the action was not met (e.g. session not running yet). |
|  500 |     13 | Internal           | Unexpected server-side failure. Safe to retry with backoff.   |
|  503 |     14 | Unavailable        | A downstream dependency was temporarily unreachable. Safe to retry with backoff. |
info.title: Bitrise Remote Dev Environments API

get.description: Retrieves all saved inputs for the current user.

parameters.description:
Whether to include secret values (e.g. secret saved-input values) in the response.
Defaults to false: secret values are redacted so they cannot accidentally leak.
Set to true only when the caller actually needs the plaintext secrets.
parameters.name: includeSecrets

post.description: Creates a new saved input (credential).

parameters.description: CreateSavedInputRequest is the request for creating a saved input.
parameters.name: body

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the saved input to delete.
parameters.name: savedInputId

get.description: Retrieves a saved input by its ID.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the saved input.
parameters.name: savedInputId

parameters.description:
Whether to include secret values (e.g. a secret saved-input value) in the response.
Defaults to false: secret values are redacted so they cannot accidentally leak.
Set to true only when the caller actually needs the plaintext secrets.
parameters.name: includeSecrets

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the saved input to update.
parameters.name: savedInputId

schema.description: UpdateSavedInputRequest is the request for updating a saved input.

get.description: Retrieves available machine images. Deprecated: use ListStacks.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

get.description: Returns workspace-level capabilities for the UI (e.g. whether the workspace is on a free trial).

parameters.description: Workspace (organization) slug. Supplied via the URL path.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

post.description: Finds clusters that offer both the given image and machine type names.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

schema.description:
ResolveClustersRequest is the request for resolving which clusters can serve
a given image + machine type combination.

get.description: Retrieves all sessions for the current user.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description:
Label selector expressions of the form "key=value". Multiple selectors
are ANDed; each is an exact-match equality on one label. At most 8
selectors; duplicate keys are rejected (they can never match under AND).
Bare keys without "=" are invalid (reserved for a future existence
operator).
parameters.name: labelSelectors

post.description: Creates a new session (remote development environment).

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

schema.description: CreateSessionRequest is the request for creating a new session.

aiPrompt.description: Optional AI prompt to pass to Claude Code when the session starts.

autoTerminateMinutes.description:
Minutes before auto-termination. Defaults to 7200 (5 days).
Set to 0 to disable auto-termination ("Never").

cluster.description:
Target cluster name. Required when the template's image + machine type are available
in multiple clusters. If only one cluster matches, this can be omitted.

enabledFeatureFlagNames.description: Names of feature flags to enable.

image.description:
Machine image name. When a template is given, optionally overrides the
template's image for this session.

Superseded by stack_id (prefer it). Accepted for now and resolved to a stack id on the fly.

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value metadata to attach to the session (e.g. team,
branch, ticket id). At most 32 entries (also enforced on the post-merge
result of updates); keys are 1-63 chars of [a-zA-Z0-9._/-] starting and
ending alphanumeric; values are 1-255 bytes of [a-zA-Z0-9._/:+-] (fits
timestamps, branches, paths). The "bitrise.io/" key prefix is reserved
for system-owned labels and rejected on user writes.

machineType.description:
Machine type name. Required when template_id is empty. When a template is
given, optionally overrides the template's machine type for this session.

mapSavedToSessionInputs.description:
When true, the backend fetches the current user's saved inputs and
auto-fills any template session inputs (matched by key) that the request
did not already supply, before required-input validation runs. The keys
that were auto-filled are reported in CreateSessionResponse.auto_mapped_inputs.

sessionInputs.description: Session input values (key/value pairs or saved input references).

stackId.description:
Stack id (e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge"). Required when template_id is empty
(unless the deprecated image is supplied). When a template is given,
optionally overrides the template's stack for this session. Preferred over image.

templateId.description:
ID of the template to use. Optional: omit to create a session without a
template. When empty, image and machine_type are required and no
warmup/startup scripts run.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to delete.
parameters.name: sessionId

get.description: Retrieves a session by its ID.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description:
Whether to include secret values (e.g. secret session-input snapshot values) in the response.
Defaults to false: secret values are redacted so they cannot accidentally leak.
Set to true only when the caller actually needs the plaintext secrets.
parameters.name: includeSecrets

patch.description: Updates a session's name or description.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to update.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: UpdateSessionRequest is the request for updating a session.

autoTerminateMinutes.description:
Updated auto-terminate duration in minutes. Resets the deadline to NOW()+minutes.
Set to 0 to disable auto-termination.

labels.description:
Labels to upsert (merged into the session's existing labels; existing keys
are overwritten, other keys are left untouched). Same constraints as
CreateSessionRequest.labels. Removals are requested via remove_labels;
when a key appears in both, the removal wins. Concurrent label updates are
last-write-wins.

removeLabels.description: Label keys to remove from the session. Unknown keys are ignored.

post.description: Clicks at the specified coordinates on the session's screen.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionClickRequest is the request for clicking at coordinates on a session's screen.

post.description: Finalizes a file upload, extracting the uploaded archive on the machine.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionCompleteFileUploadRequest is the request for completing a file upload to a session.

destinationFolder.description: Destination folder on the remote machine where files will be extracted.

uploadId.description: Upload identifier from the start upload response.

post.description: Downloads files from a session's machine as a tar.gz archive. Deprecated: use SessionDownloadFile.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
SessionDownloadRequest is the request for downloading files from a session.

Deprecated: use SessionDownloadFileRequest.

onlyContentsOfFolder.description: If true and source_path is a directory, archive only the directory contents (not the directory itself).

sourcePath.description: Absolute path on the remote machine to download (file or directory).

post.description: Downloads files from a session's machine as a tar.gz archive.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionDownloadFileRequest is the request for downloading files from a session.

onlyContentsOfFolder.description: If true and source_path is a directory, archive only the directory contents (not the directory itself).

sourcePath.description: Absolute path on the remote machine to download (file or directory).

post.description: Executes a bash command on the session's machine.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionExecuteRequest is the request for executing a command on a session.

bashCCommand.description: Bash command to execute (passed to bash -c).

get.description: Streams logs from a session's machine. The response is a server-side stream of log chunks.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description: Stage to get logs for.
parameters.name: stage

post.description: Drags the mouse between two points on the session's screen.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionMouseDragRequest is the request for dragging the mouse on a session's screen.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Session ID to list notifications for.
parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.description:
Only return notifications created before this timestamp (exclusive).
Used for backward pagination through history.
parameters.name: createdBefore

parameters.description:
Only return notifications created after this timestamp (exclusive).
Use for incremental polling after an initial fetch.
parameters.name: createdAfter

parameters.description: Maximum number of notifications to return. Default: 50, max: 100.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description:
Sort order by created_at. Default: DESC (newest first).

 - SORT_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED: Default: descending (newest first).
 - SORT_ORDER_ASC: Ascending (oldest first).
 - SORT_ORDER_DESC: Descending (newest first).
parameters.name: order

post.description: Establishes remote access (SSH/VNC) for a running session. Call this when the session status is "running" (main stage running).

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: OpenRemoteAccessRequest is the request for opening remote access to a session.

post.description: Restores a terminated session (or restarts a failed one) by creating/starting its VM.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to restore.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
RestoreSessionRequest is the request for restoring a terminated session
(also used to restart a failed session).

post.description: Takes a screenshot of the session's screen.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionScreenshotRequest is the request for taking a screenshot of a session's screen.

post.description: Scrolls at the current mouse position on the session's screen.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionScrollRequest is the request for scrolling on a session's screen.

post.description: Starts a stopped session. Deprecated: use RestoreSession.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to start.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
StartSessionRequest is the request for starting a stopped session.

Deprecated: use RestoreSessionRequest.

post.description: Initiates a file upload to a session by returning a signed PUT URL.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionStartFileUploadRequest is the request for initiating a file upload to a session.

destinationFolder.description: Destination folder on the remote machine where files will be extracted.

post.description: Initiates a file upload to a session by returning a signed PUT URL. Deprecated: use SessionStartFileUpload.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
SessionStartUploadRequest is the request for initiating a file upload to a session.

Deprecated: use SessionStartFileUploadRequest.

destinationFolder.description: Destination folder on the remote machine where files will be extracted.

post.description: Finalizes a file upload, extracting the uploaded archive on the machine. Deprecated: use SessionCompleteFileUpload.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
SessionCompleteUploadRequest is the request for completing a file upload to a session.

Deprecated: use SessionCompleteFileUploadRequest.

destinationFolder.description: Destination folder on the remote machine where files will be extracted.

uploadId.description: Upload identifier from the start upload response.

post.description: Stops a running session. Deprecated: use TerminateSession.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to stop.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description:
StopSessionRequest is the request for stopping a running session.

Deprecated: use TerminateSessionRequest.

get.description: Returns both the session's template snapshot and the current template configuration for comparison. Used to show what changed when a template is outdated.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Session ID to compare.
parameters.name: sessionId

post.description: Terminates a running session (stops the VM, preserving the session for later restore).

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session to terminate.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: TerminateSessionRequest is the request for terminating a running session.

post.description: Types text on the session's machine.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the session.
parameters.name: sessionId

schema.description: SessionTypeRequest is the request for typing text on a session.

post.description: Deletes all terminated sessions for the current user.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

schema.description: DeleteTerminatedSessionsRequest is the request for deleting all terminated sessions.

get.description: Retrieves available, provisionable stacks with friendly metadata.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description:
Whether to include secret values (e.g. secret template variables) in the response.
Defaults to false: secret values are redacted so they cannot accidentally leak.
Set to true only when the caller actually needs the plaintext secrets.
parameters.name: includeSecrets

post.description: Creates a new template configuration.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

schema.description: CreateTemplateRequest is the request for creating a new template.

description.description: Description explaining what this template is for.

image.description:
Machine image name (e.g., "osx-xcode-edge"). Consistent across clusters.

Superseded by stack_id (prefer it). Accepted for now and resolved to a stack id on the
fly so un-migrated clients keep working.

machineType.description: Machine type name (e.g., "g2.mac.m2pro.4c"). Consistent across clusters.

stackId.description:
Stack id (e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge") to store on the template. Preferred
over image. Exactly one of stack_id or image must be supplied.

startupScript.description: Startup script (bash) that runs every time the session starts or restarts.

templateVariables.description: Template variables to bake into this template.

warmupScript.description: Warmup script (bash) that runs only once during initial session creation.

workingDirectory.description: Working directory for terminal sessions (absolute path, optional).

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the template to delete.
parameters.name: templateId

get.description: Retrieves a template by its ID.

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the template.
parameters.name: templateId

parameters.description:
Whether to include secret values (e.g. secret template variables) in the response.
Defaults to false: secret values are redacted so they cannot accidentally leak.
Set to true only when the caller actually needs the plaintext secrets.
parameters.name: includeSecrets

parameters.description: Workspace ID for routing and authorization.
parameters.name: workspaceId

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the template to update.
parameters.name: templateId

schema.description: UpdateTemplateRequest is the request for updating a template.

featureFlags.description: Updated feature flags (replaces all existing when update_feature_flags is true).

image.description:
Updated image name (optional).

Superseded by stack_id (prefer it). Accepted for now and resolved to a stack id on the fly.

sessionInputs.description: Updated session inputs (replaces all existing when update_session_inputs is true).

stackId.description: Updated stack id (optional, e.g. "osx-xcode-16.0.x-edge"). Preferred over image.

templateVariables.description: Updated template variables (replaces all existing when update_template_variables is true).

updateFeatureFlags.description: Whether to update feature_flags (false = don't touch, true = replace with provided list).

updateSessionInputs.description: Whether to update session_inputs (false = don't touch, true = replace with provided list).

updateTemplateVariables.description: Whether to update template_variables (false = don't touch, true = replace with provided list).

updateWorkspaceLinks.description: Whether to update workspace_links (false = don't touch, true = replace with provided list).

workspaceLinks.description: Updated workspace links (replaces all existing when update_workspace_links is true).

BearerAuth.description: Bitrise Personal Access Token, sent as `token <PAT>`.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

tags.description: Operations on the currently authenticated user.
tags.name: User

tags.description: Workspace-level capabilities and metadata used by the UI (e.g. free-trial status).
tags.name: Workspace

tags.description: User-scoped saved credentials (e.g. GitHub PATs, SSH keys) that can be referenced from session inputs across multiple sessions.
tags.name: Saved inputs

tags.description: Reusable configurations used to create sessions. A template defines the machine image, machine type, startup/warmup scripts, template variables, session inputs, feature flags, and workspace links.
tags.name: Templates

tags.description: Lifecycle operations on remote dev environments: create, restore, terminate, list, update, delete.
tags.name: Sessions

tags.description: Operations performed against a running session's VM: shell execution, file transfer, screenshots, mouse/keyboard input, log streaming.
tags.name: Session interaction

tags.description: Lookups of available machine images, machine types, and clusters that can be used as template configuration.
tags.name: Machine catalog
